behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for a gravestone object. When the player 'looks' at the object the U8P_GRAVE_NS::look process emits a short descriptive bark ('gravestone').

When the player 'uses' or interacts with the gravestone, U8P_GRAVE_NS::use forwards execution to U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB which reads the gravestone's internal 'quality' (variant) and selects one of several hard-coded epitaph strings.

U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB starts an external Grave::read process with the chosen epitaph string, a small numeric flag (local2 = 0x1B), and the object reference ('this'), then suspends/waits. The epitaph display is therefore handled by the Grave::read process.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GRAVE_NS::look is a short process that immediately emits a single bark ('gravestone') and then suspends — used for player examination.
U8P_GRAVE_NS::use is a process entry that simply calls U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB and then suspends.

U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB queries Item::getQuality(this) to pick a branch among many hard-coded epitaph strings, then spawns Grave::read(epitaph_string, 0x1B, this) and suspends while that process runs/returns.
Control flow inside U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB is branch-heavy (many epitaph branches based on the quality/variant index) but does not modify global flags in the provided excerpt.
open_questions: Exact mapping of each Item::getQuality return value to the specific epitaph strings is present in the raw but not fully enumerated here — what all quality indices map to which messages?
What is the precise semantic meaning of the numeric flag local2 = 0x1B passed to Grave::read (display options, style, queue id, or other)?
What roles do local3 and local4 play inside U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB (they are referenced but their purpose is unclear from the excerpt)?
How Grave::read responds to the passed flag and whether it sets or checks any global state after display is not shown here.

main_function.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short, immediate description when the player examines the gravestone (emit a short bark/label).
entry_conditions: Player performs a 'U8P_GRAVE_NS::look' / examine action on the gravestone object
Engine invokes the object's U8P_GRAVE_NS::look-type process (process type 0x0208)

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_GRAVE_NS::look handler that emits a short bark ('gravestone') and suspends.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::use
function_map.role: Interaction entry point for 'U8P_GRAVE_NS::use' that calls U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB to display the epitaph.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVE_NS::func00CB
function_map.role: Selects an epitaph based on Item::getQuality(this) and starts Grave::read to show it; contains the branching logic for epitaph variants.

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate the 'U8P_GRAVE_NS::use' interaction to the epitaph-selection routine.

external_calls.purpose: Query the gravestone object's variant/quality index to decide which epitaph to display (called with 'this').
Start a process to display the selected epitaph text to the player; called with (epitaph_string, local2 = 0x1B, this). The script captures/compares the returned pid and then suspends.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for a gravestone object. When the player 'looks' at the object the U8P_GRAVE_NS::look process emits a short descriptive bark ('gravestone').
